articles.title: Who are Fernando Mendoza’s parents? What is his ethnicity, family background
articles.description: Fernando Mendoza’s No. 1 selection in the 2026 NFL Draft caps a journey shaped deeply by family. Raised by a physician father and a former athlete mother battling multiple sclerosis, Mendoza drew strength from both discipline and adversity. His emotional Heisman speech highlighted that bond. Alongside strong Cuban roots and a competitive upbringing with his brothers, Mendoza developed leadership qualities that defined Indiana’s historic season, making his rise as much personal as it is athletic.

articles.title: MI vs CSK: Sanju Samson Century Powers Chennai To Huge 103-Run Win Over Mumbai
articles.description:
IPL 2026, MI vs CSK Highlights: The "El Clasico" of IPL 2026 lived up to its billing tonight as the Chennai Super Kings (CSK) secured a dominant position against the Mumbai Indians (MI). A masterclass century from Sanju Samson served as the backbone of Chennai's innings, leaving the home side with a mountain to climb under the lights of the Wankhede Stadium.

After being put in to bat by Mumbai captain Hardik Pandya, Chennai posted a massive 207/6. Samson’s unbeaten 101 off 54 balls was the highlight of the night, featuring 10 boundaries and six towering sixes. Despite a disciplined spell from Allah Ghazanfar, the Mumbai bowlers were largely unable to stop Samson's aggressive assault.

Samson Leads the Charge

The Chennai innings began with high energy as Ruturaj Gaikwad and Sanju Samson took early control. Samson reached his second century of the IPL 2026 season in the final over, joining an elite group of players with five or more IPL hundreds. His ability to manipulate the field and target the short square boundaries kept the Mumbai fielders under constant pressure.

Mumbai's bowling attack struggled with consistency. Hardik Pandya conceded 38 runs in his two overs, while Mitchell Santner was taken for 44 in his four-over quota. Jasprit Bumrah remained the most economical of the lot, but the sheer volume of runs set a daunting target for the five-time champions.

Mumbai Indians Stumble in Chase

Chasing 208, Mumbai’s response started on a shaky note. The home side lost three quick wickets for just 46 runs within the first eight overs. Quinton de Kock fell early for 7, and the middle order faced immediate pressure from a clinical Chennai bowling unit.

Akeal Hosein was the star with the ball early on, picking up two crucial wickets while maintaining an economy rate of just 3.5. An emotional Mukesh Choudhary, playing through personal grief, also struck a vital blow by dismissing de Kock, further denting Mumbai's hopes of a successful chase.

Playoff Implications and Standings

With Mumbai currently needing 162 runs in 12 overs, the win probability has swung heavily in Chennai’s favour. A victory here would be crucial for both teams as they currently sit at the bottom of the IPL 2026 points table.

Chennai’s victory would propel them toward the mid-table, keeping their playoff dreams alive. For Mumbai, a loss at home would mean an increasingly difficult road ahead in the tournament. The match is currently in a timeout break, with Suryakumar Yadav and Tilak Varma at the crease looking to pull off an improbable recovery.

articles.title: IPL 2026 MI vs CSK Toss Update And Confirmed Playing 11
articles.description:
MI vs CSK Live: The cricket world is focused on the Wankhede Stadium as the Mumbai Indians (MI) and Chennai Super Kings (CSK) prepare for their monumental Match 33 encounter. In a crucial update, Mumbai captain Hardik Pandya has won the MI vs CSK toss and decided to bowl first, looking to exploit the evening dew later in the game.

Both franchises are desperately seeking a victory to revive their IPL 2026 campaigns. Mumbai currently sits at seventh in the rankings, just one spot ahead of Chennai. This match is viewed as a critical survival battle for both five-time champions as the race for the IPL 2026 playoffs intensifies during this mid-season phase.

MI vs CSK Toss Result: Hardik Opts to Field

Following the toss result, Hardik Pandya confirmed that Mumbai will chase, citing the red-soil nature of the Wankhede pitch and the likelihood of dew. Chasing has historically been the preferred strategy at this venue to counter the high humidity and fast outfield. Ruturaj Gaikwad mentioned he would have preferred to bowl as well but is confident in his batting unit to set a competitive target in the first innings.

Also Read: MS Dhoni Playing With 'Champak' Ahead Of MI vs CSK Clash As Fans Await Return Today - WATCH

MS Dhoni Injury Update: The Wait for Thala Continues

The primary question for millions of fans involves the MS Dhoni injury update. Despite participating in intense wicketkeeping and batting drills at the Wankhede earlier this week, the veteran keeper has not been included in the starting eleven for this clash.

Reports suggest that while he is nearing match fitness, the medical team has opted for a cautious approach. If he does not feature as an Impact Player tonight, his return is highly anticipated for Chennai's next home game at Chepauk.

Also Read: Huge Fight In PSL: Mohammad Amir’s Disgusting Send-Off To Faheem Ashraf - WATCH

MI vs CSK Final Playing 11

Both teams have finalised their lineups after dealing with significant injury concerns. The major news for the hosts is that the return of Rohit Sharma is still not possible. Rohit missed the previous matches due to a hamstring strain.

Mumbai Indians (Playing XI): Quinton de Kock(w), Naman Dhir, Suryakumar Yadav, Hardik Pandya(c), Tilak Varma, Sherfane Rutherford, Mitchell Santner, Jasprit Bumrah, Krish Bhagat, AM Ghazanfar, Ashwani Kumar

Chennai Super Kings (Playing XI): Sanju Samson(w), Ruturaj Gaikwad(c), Sarfaraz Khan, Dewald Brevis, Shivam Dube, Kartik Sharma, Jamie Overton, Noor Ahmad, Anshul Kamboj, Gurjapneet Singh, Mukesh Choudhary

articles.title: WATCH: 'I Watch Cartoons Before Match' - Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's Viral Reveal
articles.description:
Questions were initially raised about Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s age when Rajasthan Royals signed him, as he was just 13 at the time and went on to make his IPL debut at 14. However, attention has since shifted to his explosive batting and record-breaking performances.

In his debut season, he made headlines by smashing a 35-ball century - the fastest by an Indian in the IPL. While his aggressive approach continues in IPL 2026, he managed only 8 runs in the recent match against the Lucknow Super Giants.

After RR vs LSG match on Wednesday, Vaibhav spoke to a Hotstar presenter, who humorously noted that while children his age usually watch cartoons, he is instead “taking bowlers to the cleaners.” When asked what goes through his mind at the crease, Vaibhav revealed that he still maintains a simple routine.

He said, “Nothing much goes through my mind, sir. Even now, I watch cartoons every day before matches - it’s a habit I’ve not outgrown. But my focus remains on cricket. We now shift our attention to the next match, aim for two points, and move towards the playoffs.”

WATCH VIDEO

Watches cartoons, smashes sixes for fun! 😁

Not many 15-year-olds can do both. That’s Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for you! 🩷#TATAIPL #LSGvRR #LSGvsRR pic.twitter.com/sGBiMv0T8T

— Star Sports (@StarSportsIndia) April 22, 2026

In RR vs LSG match, Vaibhav was dismissed for 8 off 11 balls after being tied down by tight bowling from Mohsin Khan, including five consecutive dot balls that built pressure before his attempted big shot led to his dismissal.

Earlier, Rajasthan Royals produced a dominant bowling display to bowl out LSG for just 119, winning the match by massive margin of 40 runs. Jofra Archer starred with three wickets, while Nandre Burger and Brijesh Sharma picked up two each. Ravindra Jadeja was named Player of the Match for his all-round effort, scoring 43 runs and taking a wicket.
